programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Journeys in Japan
programme.sub-title: Kumano Kodo Fast Hiking to Nirvana
programme.desc: Michael Keida explores the inspiring beauty and mystical sacred places along the Iseji route of the Kumano Kodo while fast hiking.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Activate Your Japanese!
programme.sub-title: I want us to be friends
programme.desc: Learn how to make the most of your Japanese skills. Watch our drama that features useful communication strategies, our snappy videos about onomatopoeia, and our documentaries on "senpai" in Japan.

programme.title: Dig More Japan
programme.desc: The shinkansen: almost never late. Lost items: nearly always recovered. Japan's railway hospitality is legendary. Let's ride the rails and dig a little deeper.

programme.title: Dining with the Chef mini
programme.desc: Featured recipe: Ginger Beef Steak/ Learn about Japanese home cooking with Master Chef Saito, based on traditional Japanese cooking techniques in five minutes!

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Dining with the Chef
programme.desc: Onigiri-Japanese rice balls-are a classic home-cooked dish that anyone can make. Chef Rika and an expert show us ways to make delicious onigiri, starting from the beginning: cooking the rice.

programme.title: NEWSROOM TOKYO
programme.desc: NEWSROOM TOKYO dives deeper into the day's news with insight and analysis, from the perspective of Japan and the rest of Asia. We put a spotlight on what you need to know to understand the issues.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: A Device That Improves Children's Vision
programme.desc: Amblyopia is a condition where vision in one eye doesn't properly develop. Ishigaki Yo has created a device that improves vision through gaming. He talks about designing products that benefit society.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN 5min.
programme.desc: A fantastic tale of love for local flavor, set in Kurume, birthplace of tonkotsu ramen.

programme.title: On the Wings 5min.
programme.desc: Hop on a plane and view Japan from the sky! It's nearly the apex of summer, and we're heading to the nature rich Amami Oshima Island, which recently became a UNESCO World Natural Heritage site.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE
programme.desc: We visit an Edo-style open-air teahouse, where the townspeople enjoyed refreshments, conversation ... and the company of exotic birds. For them, these outings were about more than a good time.

programme.title: Japan in Focus
programme.sub-title: A Gift from the Stars: 10 Years After the Kumamoto Earthquakes
programme.desc: Ten years ago, massive earthquakes struck Kumamoto. We follow the stories of people who, stripped of their former lives by the disaster, found inspiration in the stars to get back on their feet.

programme.title: Document 72 hours
programme.sub-title: A Mountain Water Spring in Wintry Nagano
programme.desc: People from far and wide flock to a spring deep in the Nagano Prefecture mountains that offers free water. For three days in early winter, we asked them why this water is worth coming for.

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.sub-title: The Beauty and Beliefs of the Shinto Shrine on the Sea: Itsukushima Shrine

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: At the Niihama Taiko Matsuri, teams of around 150 people carry taikodai: three-tonne parade floats housing a huge taiko drum. They compete to give the most powerful synchronized performance.

programme.title: JAPAN DELISH
programme.desc: "Curry rice," one of Japan's most iconic national dishes. How did Jimbocho, Tokyo become a curry town?

programme.sub-title: A World Heritage Town at Risk: Laos

programme.desc: Mt. Fuji, the tallest mountain in Japan, has many faces. See the beauty of these different Fujis through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: IMPACTS: Climates Change the World
programme.sub-title: COP30 in Brazil: Episode 4 Climate Change and the Global Food Crisis
programme.desc: Global warming leads to widespread famine. By the end of the century, it is predicted that yields of maize, soybeans, and wheat may fall by 30-40%. We examine the scientific data on this global issue.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: Will Sparks Fly?
programme.sub-title: A Glance at a Potential Future
programme.desc: A dapper young man sits in a teahouse with his older companion when a young woman catches his eye. From the way they're looking at each other, it's clear that there's something going on between them.

programme.title: Medical Frontiers
programme.sub-title: New Ways to Slow Alzheimer's Disease
programme.desc: Two drugs have been approved for their potential to slow the progression of Alzheimer's disease. We look at how they work and report on new findings suggesting a food-derived component may also help.

programme.title: Trailblazers
programme.desc: Tiara designer Kamitani Taro specializes in custom pieces. He's now working with a newly developed material, and we follow his creative process to see how he puts the sparkle into his designs.

programme.desc: Tiara designer Kamitani Taro specializes in custom pieces. He's now working with a newly developed material, and we follow his creative process to see how he puts the sparkle into his designs.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: CYCLE AROUND JAPAN 15min.
programme.desc: A series showcasing the stunning landscapes featured in Cycle Around Japan. This time: Awajishima, in the Seto Inland Sea, plus Niijima and Hachijojima, Pacific islands with a tropical feel.

programme.title: Design X Stories
programme.desc: Today's theme: Vanishing Tools. As the rising age of artisans puts the survival of certain crafts at risk, we shine a light on the vanishing tools used to keep them alive, and their anonymous designs.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Train Cruise
programme.desc: Spring arrives late in eastern Hokkaido. Travel from Obihiro through Japan's food base and along the Pacific Ocean to Nemuro. Taste local delicacies, drive a train, and explore the vast marshlands.

programme.title: Japanology Plus mini
programme.desc: Japan is a hot spot for discovering dinosaur fossils. An excavation in Hokkaido sparked a nationwide enthusiasm for dinosaurs, leading to entertainment such as dinosaur robots and mechanical suits.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: SAKE TRUTH
programme.desc: We explore various truths about sake from the koji that determines the taste to medieval innovations that changed sake-making. We also examine the science behind why sake changes flavor when heated.

programme.title: CINEMATIC JOURNEY
programme.desc: Daniel Moore takes a cinematic journey through Nagano. In springtime he hits the south, experiencing the breathtaking cherry blossoms, and in winter gets lost in magical winter landscapes up north.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE(5)
programme.desc: Mornings begin early for fishmongers and vegetable peddlers at Nihonbashi Bridge, and here is a reason. They are not going to bow to the lord's procession. The bustling scene from everyday Edo life.

programme.title: BOSAI: Science that Can Save Your
programme.desc: The historic city of Kyoto faces threats of landslides and post-earthquake fires. What modern systems defend this ancient city from natural disasters?

programme.title: DESIGN MUSEUM JAPAN
programme.desc: Tagawa Kinya (Design engineer)investigates the process of design by looking at the work of Yanagi Sori, who shaped products by making models using different materials.

programme.title: KABUKI KOOL
programme.desc: Actor Kataoka Ainosuke explores the sometimes tragic and occasionally comical relationships between married couples in kabuki. Explore these dramatic tales of fidelity and romance.

programme.title: The Isolado Reemerge

programme.title: Darwin's Amazing Animals
programme.desc: It's the world's fastest land animal. But that rarely helps in Africa's bush where the cheetah thrives. Let's discover some of its amazing hunting techniques, including what looks like teleporting!

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: The Amakusa region has over 120 islands and is home to one of the places where a unique form of Christianity developed. See Amakusa's historic sights and natural beauty through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: Rockie and Her Friends
programme.desc: Puppet action show for children. In a distant post-human future where surviving creatures have formed a community, new-breed dinosaur girl Rockie sets out on hilarious adventures with her schoolmates.

programme.title: TAKE TECH
programme.desc: A program for upper primary to lower secondary school children to explore the everyday technologies all around them. Each episode takes a piece of familiar tech to find out what it does and how.

programme.title: Little Charo

programme.sub-title: Nara's Todaiji Temple: Deer and Cats

programme.title: The World Heritage Sites in Japan
programme.sub-title: 250 Years of Faithful Prayer: Hidden Christian Sites in the Nagasaki Region
programme.desc: After Christianity was outlawed in Japan during the 17th century, Japanese Christians went into hiding. Explore the ruins where some made their last stand, as well as the beautiful Oura Cathedral.

programme.title: Nun's Cookbook
programme.desc: The temple chefs use freshly picked bamboo shoots to prepare chirashizushi. This springtime treat is the perfect addition to the cherry blossom viewing party menu.

programme.title: Dive in Tokyo
programme.sub-title: Tateishi - A Riverside Town of Creative Manufacturing
programme.desc: Tateishi is a riverside neighborhood in eastern Tokyo that developed as a center of manufacturing. We learn about the local dyeing and toy industries and explore an old-fashioned shopping arcade.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: The highlight of Onabare is nerikomi, which involves the careful wielding of a long, heavy pole. This follows the parade of a portable shrine through the streets with performances along the way.

programme.title: JAPAN DELISH 5min.
programme.desc: Ever heard of an-pan?! From fruit sandwiches to curry-filled delights, Japanese bread is full of delicious surprises!

programme.title: Time-lapse Japan
programme.desc: *First broadcast on January 5, 2026 Hagi, a World Heritage site often described as an "open-air museum," is lined with merchant and samurai houses and retains much of its 17th-century townscape. Soak in the atmosphere, in time-lapse.

programme.title: Spiritual Explorers
programme.sub-title: Mt. Gassan - Climbing as a Form of Prayer
programme.desc: Gassan, revered as a sacred peak for 1,400 years, is where ancestral spirits are said to reside. Our explorer joins worshippers who climb to be with the dead.

programme.title: A Cat's-Eye View of Japan
programme.desc: We take in Japan's tallest mountain, Mt. Fuji, and meet some cats on the way. A cat named Bond carries out top-secret missions at a peach orchard. Another cat patrols its home—a 100-year-old winery.

programme.desc: A 15-minute spin-off of the "Somewhere Street" series, "A Short Stroll" explores cities through a traveler's eyes.Today we visit Perth in Australia.

programme.title: The Masterpieces of Ukiyo-e producer Tsutaju
programme.sub-title: The Actor Otani Oniji III as Edobei
programme.desc: This miniseries presents the masterpieces of Tsutaju, a brilliant ukiyo-e producer of the Edo period. This episode spotlights artist Sharaku's debut work "The Actor Otani Oniji III as Edobei."

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: In 2025, Yasuda Kan won the Isamu Noguchi Award, given to artists who embody a spirit of innovation, global consciousness, and commitment to cultural exchange. He talks about the power of sculpture.

programme.title: Japan's Top Inventions
programme.desc: Discover the stories behind renowned Japanese products alongside our reporter. This time: automatic floodgates.

programme.title: NHK NEWSLINE
programme.desc: NHK NEWSLINE brings you up to date with the latest from Japan, Asia and around the world. Our team covers breaking news and major developments, with trusted anchors to tie it all together.

programme.title: Cinematic Journey
programme.desc: A scientific trek into Earth's memory. See Anami's ancient strata, the singing sands of Kotobikihama, and the geological time gaps of Uradome's rocks.

programme.title: Time-lapse Journey
programme.desc: Kamikochi is a beautiful mountainous region in Nagano Prefecture that's 1,500m above sea level. With a mirror-like pond and rime ice, see the frozen beauty of Kamikochi through time-lapse photography.

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.desc: Ito Satomi has designed costumes for many top-tier figure skaters from Japan and overseas. She shares her passion and inspiration for her craft.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN 5min.
programme.desc: A whirlwind tour of Hokkaido's best ramen! Why not whet your appetite with the origin story of Sapporo miso ramen?

programme.title: On the Wings
programme.desc: Fly high from Shizuoka Airport, the nearest to Mt. Fuji! Breathtaking window views and special cockpit footage await!

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: A Stylish Concert
programme.sub-title: New Year's Concert in the Inner Chambers
programme.desc: We attend a New Year's concert at a samurai residence, where a master koto player delights a crowd of gorgeously dressed celebrity-types. Lounging in the best seat in the house is no ordinary V.I.P.

programme.title: Science View
programme.desc: At Mt. Fuji's 3,776-meter summit, scientists study clouds, lightning, and climate. Vanguard Researchers features a mind-controlled prosthetic arm.

programme.title: FRONTRUNNERS
programme.sub-title: Master Sculptor of Buddhist Statues: Matsumoto Shukan
programme.desc: Matsumoto Myokei, a "once-in-a-century" sculptor of Buddhist statues, is passing his skills onto his grandson, Shukan. 80 years on from the atomic bomb attacks, Shukan sculpts a message of peace.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: Onomichi Betcha is a three-day festival in Hiroshima Prefecture. Masked ogres carry sticks with which to "torment" spectators. But being hit or poked with a stick is said to bring good luck.

programme.title: Herbal Symphony #2 Summer
programme.desc: In Uda, discover Japan's oldest private medicinal herb garden, where the natural landscape from 300 years ago is preserved in a botanical time capsule, a sanctuary of the local culture and history.

programme.title: Satoyama Kitchen Diary
programme.desc: Hi! It's me, Tama. Spring's here! A season for tasty eating! But first, let me introduce my humans. Grandma Nobuko cooks and Grandpa Makoto films. Today, I hear they're preparing something...with camellias!?

programme.title: Direct Talk
programme.sub-title: Sharing a Unique Voice with the World
programme.desc: In the choir division of a major gospel event, held in New York in May 2025, TiA led a group of 86 Japanese singers to victory. She tells us about the significance of gospel music.

programme.title: RAMEN JAPAN 5min.
programme.desc: A fantastic tale of love for local flavor, set in Kurume, birthplace of tonkotsu ramen.

programme.title: On the Wings 5min.
programme.desc: Hop on a plane and view Japan from the sky! It's nearly the apex of summer, and we're heading to the nature rich Amami Oshima Island, which recently became a UNESCO World Natural Heritage site.

programme.title: Ukiyoe EDO-LIFE: A Stylish Concert
programme.sub-title: New Year's Concert in the Inner Chambers
programme.desc: We attend a New Year's concert at a samurai residence, where a master koto player delights a crowd of gorgeously dressed celebrity-types. Lounging in the best seat in the house is no ordinary V.I.P.

programme.title: MATSURI: The Heartbeat of Japan
programme.desc: Higashinada Danjiri involves a parade of decorated floats, pulled by teams of veterans and ridden by young performers that lean out over the crowd. 32 districts take part with their own float.

programme.title: Nun's Cookbook
programme.desc: After preparing fresh bamboo shoots, the chefs add seasoning and potato starch, then deep fry until the color is a golden brown. This crispy dish is the perfect springtime treat.
